title: The Secret-Sharing Cabinet
excerpt: Pure-Rust implementations of every constructive secret-sharing scheme from the foundational papers — Shamir, Blakley, McEliece-Sarwate, Asmuth-Bloom, Brickell, Karchmer-Wigderson, Naor-Shamir visual cryptography, and the rest.

title: Testing the Untestable: A Statistical Audit Suite for RNGs
excerpt: A pure-Rust harness that runs NIST SP 800-22, DIEHARD, and DIEHARDER against 43 generators — from deliberately broken historical PRNGs to ChaCha20 and the known-backdoored Dual_EC_DRBG.

title: Cryptography From the Specifications
excerpt: A pure-Rust crate that implements classical and modern cryptographic primitives directly from their published specifications — auditable, portable, and written so you can read it.

title: On Time
excerpt: The only thing that you truly have is time. Money is not time — money is what people give you in exchange for your time.

title: Performance Evaluation: Do It Right or Don't Do It
excerpt: Bad performance evaluation is endemic in systems research. It is not hard to do it correctly — so why don't we?

title: Ercole Bottani
excerpt: An electrical engineer from Volpago del Montello, and the start of my academic lineage.

title: Luigi Dadda
excerpt: From analog circuits to the Dadda multiplier, and Italy's first digital computer.

title: Domenico Ferrari
excerpt: From Politecnico di Milano to Berkeley — performance evaluation, BSD Unix, and Internet QoS.

title: Angelo Barbagelata
excerpt: An electrical engineer from Novi Ligure who designed and tested most of the electrical installations in Northern Italy.

title: Jehan-François Pâris
excerpt: From Belgium to Berkeley to Houston, with stops at Purdue and UC San Diego — my doctoral advisor.
